semanticCore: Forelock, front hair, forehead, grasp, or leader
quranicRelevance: Grasping by the forelock (Nasiyah - 'akhithun bi-nasiyatiha', 'lanasfa'an bin-nasiyati').

keyDerivations.meaning: Forelock, front hair, forehead, or crown of the head

figurativeOrRareUsages: نَاصِيَةٍ كَاذِبَةٍ خَاطِئَةٍ (96:16): A lying, sinning forelock (attribution of character traits to the brain's front lobe).
summaryEn: The root refers to the forelock (Nasiyah), representing submission (God holding creation's forelocks) and the drag of the lying, sinning forelock of Abu Jahl.
summaryAr: يشير هذا الجذر إلى شعر مقدمة الرأس (ناصية)، معبراً عن مطلق خضوع الخلائق تحت تدبير الله (آخذ بناصيتها)، وجر ناصية الطغاة الكاذبة لعذاب النار.
rawText: تَنَصَّى بَنِى فُلاَنٍ 5 : see تَذَرَّى. نَاصِيَةٌ properly, in the language of the [classical] Arabs, The place where the hair grows in the fore part of the head: and hence, the hair of that part; the hair over the forehead; (Az, TA;) [and this is the general meaning;) i. q. قُصَّةٌ and طُرَّةٌ. (Msb, art. قص.) The forelock of a horse. === نَاصِيَةُ بَنِى فُلاَنٍ: see تَذَرَّى and ذِرْوَةٌ. نَصِىٌّ A certain plant: (S, TA:) Golius says, a species of thistle; but this seems to be inconsistent with the description of it: see رَيِّجَةٌ. === خَصِىٌّ نَصِيٌّ Gelded, castrated. The second word is an imitative sequent.
